<!-- Generated by GSD docs-update -->

# Getting Started

This guide walks you through running Screws Box locally from source and verifying everything works.

## Prerequisites

- **Go 1.22+** (the project uses Go 1.26.1; any 1.22+ should work)
- **Git**

No external databases or services are required. SQLite is embedded and sessions default to in-memory storage.

## Clone and Build

```bash
git clone <your-repo-url>
cd screws-box
go build -o screws-box ./cmd/screwsbox
```

This produces a single static binary (`CGO_ENABLED=0` by default via the pure-Go SQLite driver).

## Run

```bash
./screws-box
```

The server starts on [http://localhost:8080](http://localhost:8080).

On first launch, you will be prompted to create admin credentials (username and password). These are stored in the local SQLite database at `./screws_box.db`.

## First Steps

1. **Open the grid** -- Navigate to [http://localhost:8080](http://localhost:8080). You'll see a chessboard-style grid representing your organizer (default: 10 columns x 5 rows).

2. **Add an item** -- Click any cell (e.g., "3B") to open the container panel. Click "Add Item", enter a name like "M6 washer", optionally add a description and tags (`m6`, `washer`), then save.

3. **Search** -- Type in the search bar at the top. Matching containers highlight on the grid as you type. Select tags from the filter dropdown to narrow results.

4. **Admin panel** -- Click "Admin" in the header to access shelf settings, auth configuration, OIDC setup, session management, and data export/import.

## Configuration

All configuration is via environment variables. The most common:

```bash
PORT=3000 ./screws-box              # change listen port
DB_PATH=/data/app.db ./screws-box   # change database location
```

See [docs/CONFIGURATION.md](CONFIGURATION.md) for the full reference.

## Dev Mode

For development with live template/static file reloading (no rebuild needed):

```bash
go run -tags dev ./cmd/screwsbox
```

See [docs/DEVELOPMENT.md](DEVELOPMENT.md) for the full development workflow.

## Docker

If you prefer Docker over building from source:

```bash
docker compose up -d
```

This starts Screws Box on port 8080 with data persisted in `./data/`. See [docs/DEPLOYMENT.md](DEPLOYMENT.md) for production deployment options.
